title: Audit Of Privileged Access Semantic Contract
description: Source-backed draft semantic contract for the audit of privileged access capability, derived from historical source-lineage evidence as lineage. Facets cite verified source-import manifest rows; proof is hermetic/runtime lineage and not fresh USF proof.
aiGuidance: Source-backed draft. Facets cite verified manifest source rows as lineage; proof is hermetic/stale and not current readiness. Do not treat as implementation authority or fresh proof; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: The privileged-audit lifecycle is a read-only contextual query over durable audit rows: choose a logical resource, map it to the stored resource and per-context permission, query tenant-scoped audit events, redact secret metadata, and return a bounded result. It consumes the audit evidence produced by privileged operations such as support/break-glass access.

stateModel.description: State is an audit-event projection carrying id, action, actor id, resource, resource id, timestamp, and redacted metadata, where logical resources map to member, config, feature, and auth-settings contexts. The capability projects existing durable rows rather than owning new mutable state.

permissions.description: The route requires a coarse tenant audit-read permission and the usecase additionally enforces a per-context permission such as member, config, feature, or auth-settings read. The tenant id comes from the resolved session, never from the client, preserving tenant isolation.

contracts.description: The operation surface is a single tenant-scoped audit query accepting resource, resource id, action, actor, from/to, and limit parameters. It returns a bounded, redacted projection.

validation.description: Unknown logical resources are invalid, a missing per-context permission is forbidden, the limit is clamped to a bounded range, and resource-id, action, actor, and date filters are optional. Invalid queries return no rows.

errorModel.description: Typed outcomes are ok, forbidden, and invalid, mapped fail-closed to 200, 403, and 400; provider failure returns a typed degraded result. The query never returns cross-tenant or unauthorised rows.

auditModel.description: The capability reads existing audit events and is not itself a mutation audit, redacting secret-like metadata keys and not exposing client IP or user agent. It is the consumer side of the privileged-access audit dependency, failing closed when producer evidence is unavailable.

readinessModel.description: Readiness is invariant-ready for contextual audit reads, and degraded mode keeps read-only projection visible while disabling any dependent mutation. A broader live history proof validates read-only projection, source isolation, secret exclusion, pagination, and absence of mutation against the live data substrate.

proof.description: A unit proof covers the contextual audit semantics and an adjacent runtime history proof validates live read-only audit/history projection, demonstrated in source lineage via hermetic/runtime validation. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: Audit panels with logical-resource, action, actor, and time filters, pagination, forbidden/invalid states, and redacted metadata.
